title: euphorbia hamata var. hamata
intro: Euphorbia hamata var. hamata, also known as the thorny spurge, is a type of succulent plant native to tropical Africa, characterized by its unique, thorny stems and small, green leaves. The medicinal properties of Euphorbia hamata var. hamata are attributed to its sap, which is rich in latex containing euphorbon, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. This plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, thanks to its health benefits such as reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting wound healing. Euphorbia hamata var. hamata can be used in various herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
